The first time, the SSA told me I owed them $12,572 and asked me to pay it off. I told them I didn't have the money. They asked if I had a credit card; I only had a debit card with about $500 in the bank. They deducted two months of my SSDI and SSI payments, about $1300. After that, the SSA withheld 10% of my SSDI and SSI payments. I appealed, and they stopped withholding 10% of my SSDI, but continued withholding 10% of my SSI for over a year and a half, totaling about $1500.  They withheld over $3000 in total and sent me a notice saying I still owed $10,568 and that they would continue withholding 10% of my benefits for 10 years, plus fees.

I know that suing the SSA in Minnesota is very dangerous, but I only have one life, no wife or children, so I have nothing more to lose.
The SSA office in Minnesota sent me a complaint form for the third time, giving me 60 days to resolve the issue of them withholding 10% of my SSDI & SSI payments. If they refuse, I will go to the office and submit a petition requesting a resolution or a hearing. I will go with a representative from the healthcare office and demand that they handle my case. Hennepin County Medical Assistance asked why the SSA was withholding 10% of my SSDI & SSI without allowing me to appeal or telling me to appeal. I filed the complaint, but it was rejected without the SSA giving me a reason. I filed the first appeal in January 2025, they received the appeal in March 2025, and then told me to go home and they would resolve it. The second time, in March 2025, they received my application in June 2025, but didn't respond. I went directly to the Social Security office and they said the government was shut down. I said that the government shutdown was from September 30, 2025 to November 13, 2025, which had nothing to do with my appeal. They sent me a letter telling me to appeal again on December 23, 2025. They said I had to wait 60 days for a resolution. If they don't resolve it the third time, I will call a representative from the healthcare program directly and they will have to answer why the SSA told me to file a complaint but didn't resolve it.
SSA office is holding  10 % my benefit  SSDI & SSI   they sent letter to me for my appealing  but they denied to  hearing my case  3 time

I went with an American woman who works in a health insurance program to the SSA office in Minneapolis, MN. They asked if I needed an interpreter. I told them I was speaking English with my friend.  They asked if I needed her to translate for the SSA staff to understand better. They required a representative when filing a complaint.

The SSA staff said they would contact the representative before meeting with me for a hearing. I asked why I couldn't participate in the conversation. They said there were necessary basic rules. They didn't let me hear anything. The insurance woman told me to answer only when asked and not to talk about politics or corruption. I told her I would lose my social security benefits if I didn't speak up; who would help me get back the money that had been withheld for over a year and a half?
